skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guide and helper tooling for designing, initializing, validating, and packaging AI skills with reusable resources and clear instructions.
skill.summary: Create and package AI skills with structured guidance and helper scripts.
skill.author: AbdullahMalik17

security_audit.summary: Most static findings are false positives from Markdown examples, Apache License text, and template prose. The real risk is moderate filesystem exposure because the packager archives every file under the chosen skill directory.

medium_findings.title: Archive Packaging May Include Unintended Files
medium_findings.description: The packager iterates over all files under the selected skill directory and writes them into the .skill archive. This is legitimate packaging behavior, but it can expose secrets if the input directory contains sensitive files or symlinked content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem packaging behavior is directly present and affects all files under the chosen path. I did not find malicious intent, so confidence is moderate rather than high.

low_findings.title: Apache License URLs Are Not Network Activity
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to standard Apache License text. The files do not make network requests or transmit user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are license references, not executable code. No network library or request call is present at these locations.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Are Not Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown fences, inline filenames, or examples showing where scripts belong. They do not execute commands by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is documentation or template text. I found no subprocess call, shell invocation, eval, or dynamic execution path.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Findings Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak-crypto locations are natural language, license text, or template descriptions. No cryptographic library or hashing function is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanner appears to match short substrings inside ordinary prose. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or related implementation appears in the inspected files.

low_findings.title: Validation Script Does Not Access Credentials
low_findings.description: The validator reads SKILL.md, parses YAML with safe_load, and checks allowed frontmatter keys. It does not read environment variables, certificates, or private keys.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant code reads a local Markdown file and validates metadata. No certificate path, key material, environment access, or reconnaissance command is present.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Is Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The scanner combined license URLs, Markdown examples, and local file operations into a critical heuristic. No credential access, obfuscation, exfiltration, or command execution was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The underlying signals are individually explainable as documentation, licensing, or local packaging. I found no semantic evidence of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unfiltered Archive File Inclusion
dangerous_patterns.description: The package script archives every regular file discovered under the skill path. This pattern should be used only on trusted, reviewed skill directories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicit in the archive loop. The risk depends on the directory contents and is not evidence of malware by itself.

content.value_statement: Building reusable AI skills is hard when structure, triggers, and packaging rules are unclear. This skill guides design, scaffolding, validation, and packaging.
actual_capabilities: Explains required skill structure, including SKILL.md frontmatter and bundled resources.
Guides authors through planning reusable scripts, references, and assets.
Provides an initializer script that creates a starter skill directory.
Provides a validator for SKILL.md frontmatter and naming rules.
Provides a packager that creates a .skill archive after validation.
Includes reference patterns for workflows and consistent output formats.
limitations: It does not publish skills to a marketplace or remote registry.
Validation is limited to basic structure and frontmatter checks.
The packager includes all files under the selected skill directory.
Authors must still test domain-specific scripts and references themselves.

use_cases.title: Create a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Plan triggers, reusable resources, and a clear SKILL.md for a new internal workflow.

use_cases.title: Package a Finished Skill
use_cases.description: Validate structure and create a distributable .skill archive for review or sharing.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Instructions
use_cases.description: Refactor an existing skill to reduce context load and move details into references.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a new skill for this task: [describe task]. Identify the trigger description, core workflow, and needed resources.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Skill Resources
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these example user requests and decide which content belongs in SKILL.md, references, scripts, or assets.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Skill Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill directory for structure, frontmatter quality, resource organization, and packaging risks before I distribute it.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an Advanced Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this existing skill for trigger precision, progressive disclosure, duplicated context, missing tests, and unsafe bundled resources.

output_examples.input: I need a skill for recurring PDF form workflows.
output_examples.output: A concise skill outline with trigger wording, workflow steps, script recommendations, and validation tasks.

output_examples.input: My skill has long instructions and many examples.
output_examples.output: A refactoring plan that keeps essential guidance in SKILL.md and moves detailed examples into reference files.

output_examples.input: I want to package this skill for review.
output_examples.output: A preflight checklist covering validation, test evidence, secret removal, and archive contents.

best_practices: Keep SKILL.md focused on triggers, workflow, and navigation.
Move long examples and reference material into separate files.
Review archive contents before sharing a packaged skill.
anti_patterns: Do not add unrelated README, changelog, or installation files to a skill.
Do not hide important trigger conditions only inside the body.
Do not package directories that contain secrets, credentials, or unrelated files.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create AI skills with clear triggers, structured instructions, and reusable bundled resources.

faq.question: Does it create a complete skill automatically?
faq.answer: It can scaffold a starter directory, but the author must write domain-specific guidance and test any scripts.

faq.question: Can it validate a skill before packaging?
faq.answer: Yes. The included validator checks frontmatter, naming rules, and basic structure.

faq.question: Does the packager upload files anywhere?
faq.answer: No. It creates a local .skill archive and does not perform network uploads.

faq.question: What should I check before packaging?
faq.answer: Check that the directory contains only intended files and no secrets, credentials, or private data.

faq.question: Is this only for Claude?
faq.answer: No. The concepts apply to Claude, Codex, and Claude Code skill authoring workflows.
